talks.title: Civil Work: Putting Syrian refugees back on their feet
talks.speaker: Rasha Maaty
talks.speakerBio: Rasha Maaty is a social impact advocate focused on humanitarian work and refugee support. Her work centers on creating sustainable livelihoods and integration programs for displaced communities.

talks.title: Restructuring our living spaces through container architecture
talks.speaker: Youssef Farag
talks.speakerBio: Youssef Farag is an architect and innovator exploring sustainable and affordable housing solutions through creative architectural design. His work utilizes recycled and unconventional materials to address housing challenges.

talks.title: Can we still say that there are plenty of fish in the sea by 2050?
talks.speaker: Nada El Shanawany
talks.speakerBio: Nada El Shanawany is a marine biologist and environmental scientist dedicated to studying and protecting marine ecosystems. Her research addresses the critical challenges facing ocean sustainability and biodiversity.

talks.title: Drama Therapy: An unlikely solution to a common problem
talks.speaker: Reem Hashem
talks.speakerBio: Reem Hashem is a drama therapist and mental health professional who uses theatrical techniques and creative expression as therapeutic tools. She pioneered drama therapy programs addressing psychological and emotional challenges in diverse populations.

talks.title: A biomarker saving lives
talks.speaker: Rasha Hanafi
talks.speakerBio: Rasha Hanafi is a biomedical researcher and healthcare innovator focused on developing diagnostic tools and biomarkers to improve patient outcomes. Her research bridges the gap between laboratory discovery and clinical application.

talks.title: Mental illness representation in Egyptian movies
talks.speaker: Caroline Mitry
talks.speakerBio: Caroline Mitry is a media studies scholar and cultural critic examining the portrayal of mental health issues in film and television. Her work advocates for more accurate and stigma-reducing representation of mental illness in media.

talks.title: A sustainable development model for red sea rural areas
talks.speaker: Omar Sabaa
talks.speakerBio: Omar Sabaa is a development expert and environmental consultant specializing in sustainable community development in rural and coastal regions. His work integrates economic growth with environmental conservation and social welfare.

talks.title: Post chemotherapy: Getting ovaries back to life
talks.speaker: Sara Abdel-Aziz
talks.speakerBio: Sara Abdel-Aziz is a reproductive health specialist and oncology advocate focused on fertility preservation and quality of life for cancer survivors. Her work addresses the overlooked intersection of cancer treatment and reproductive health.

talks.title: Research: The marginalized dimension in our fight against cancer
talks.speaker: Yehia Abugabal
talks.speakerBio: Yehia Abugabal is a cancer researcher and public health advocate emphasizing the importance of diverse and inclusive research in improving cancer treatment outcomes. His work highlights underrepresented populations in medical research.

talks.title: Beyond limits, different is beautiful
talks.speaker: Rania Abdelaziz & Alaa El Masry
talks.speakerBio: Rania Abdelaziz and Alaa El Masry are advocates for inclusion and accessibility. Their work focuses on challenging perceptions about disability and celebrating human diversity.

talks.title: Reclaiming Egypt's narrative
talks.speaker: Mahmoud Hawary
talks.speakerBio: Mahmoud Hawary is a cultural historian and filmmaker dedicated to preserving and promoting Egypt's rich historical narratives.

talks.title: From machines to bodies
talks.speaker: Yahya Hefnawi
talks.speakerBio: Yahya Hefnawi is an engineer and innovator exploring the intersection of technology and human physiology.

talks.title: Getting companies racing to hire you
talks.speaker: Youssef El Akkari
talks.speakerBio: Youssef El Akkari is a career coach and talent development specialist helping young professionals stand out.

talks.title: Bridging the professional gap: The transformative power of network
talks.speaker: Ahmed El Helaly
talks.speakerBio: Ahmed El Helaly is a professional development coach emphasizing the transformative impact of strategic professional relationships.

talks.title: It didn't start with you
talks.speaker: Shereen Rizk
talks.speakerBio: Shereen Rizk is a psychotherapist exploring generational trauma and healing.

talks.title: Creating movie magic!
talks.speaker: Dibo
talks.speakerBio: Dibo is a filmmaker and visual effects artist passionate about bringing stories to life.

talks.title: Bringing the past to life
talks.speaker: Rowan El Selmy
talks.speakerBio: Rowan El Selmy is an archaeologist dedicated to uncovering and preserving historical narratives.

talks.title: Goal Of Equality
talks.speaker: Ali Aboulnasr
talks.speakerBio: Ali Aboulnasr is a social justice advocate focused on equality and civic impact.

talks.title: STEM-ED
talks.speaker: Mohamed Rizkallah
talks.speakerBio: Mohamed Rizkallah is an education and technology speaker who explores the intersection of STEM and learning.

talks.title: Exposing hidden dimensions of Child Sexual Abuse.
talks.speaker: Sara Aziz
talks.speakerBio: Sara Aziz is a health advocate raising awareness about child safety and support systems.

talks.title: Criminals in Suits
talks.speaker: Sue Ellen Hassouna
talks.speakerBio: Sue Ellen Hassouna is a speaker exploring ethics, justice, and the hidden realities behind appearances.

talks.title: Art therapy: a world beyond creative expression
talks.speaker: Carol Hammal
talks.speakerBio: Carol Hammal is an art therapy advocate who uses creativity as a path to healing and expression.
